Open records tips

November 1997
Investigative Methods

This article provides practical tips for journalists navigating open records act requests, including being specific about data needs, personalizing communications to school administrators, and maintaining detailed documentation of all follow-up efforts.

Leavell's tips for successful open records act compliance:

Know exactly what data you need. Tailor specific questionnaires for schools to fill out. Send the letters directly to administrators by name rather than title. Enclose a self-addressed, stamped envelope and a fax number. Keep a detailed log of each follow-up call you've made to the school, including names of people you talked to, when you called and what they said. Enclose a state attorney general's decision about a similar open records case with each letter.
